title: Trim47 Pathway Targeting for Blood-Brain Barrier Restoration in Cystic Encephalomalacia

input.description: Trim47 (E3 ubiquitin ligase) provides neuroprotection in hypoxic-ischemic brain injury by preserving blood-brain barrier integrity through interaction with tight junction protein ZO1. In HIE with ongoing barrier dysfunction contributing to cystic encephalomalacia progression, Trim47 upregulation could stabilize residual barrier function and reduce secondary injury cascades. While no direct Trim47 agonist exists clinically, pathway analysis suggests potential targets: compounds that reduce Trim47 degradation, enhance its expression, or mimic its ZO1-stabilizing effects. This represents a novel mechanistic target where barrier restoration could slow cystic progression and create a more permissive environment for regenerative therapies.
